response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and web developer with over 15 years of experience, but is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The single most disqualifying factor is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency, which is listed as a hard native-level requirement with no stated flexibility. Beyond this blocker, the candidate has no demonstrable experience in embedded networking security, LAN-switch firmware development, Linux kernel modification, or relevant security protocols such as MACsec or IEEE 802.1X. While C/C++ appears in the skills list, it is backed only by a brief stint at National Instruments and a student thesis project, falling far short of the deep embedded-C expertise the role demands.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience spanning web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded/hardware contexts
Hands-on Linux experience and low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic using async I/O and high-performance architectures
Exposure to REST APIs, networked service development, and some hardware control work at Altom Consulting
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — a listed hard native-level requirement that cannot be waived
No experience with embedded networking security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, Linux kernel source modification)
Core expertise is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB) with only superficial and dated C/C++ exposure

key_factors.factor: No embedded security or networking domain experience
